provisions.summary: 15-year term from Effective Date. Single location only. Must continuously operate once opened.

No exclusivity. Franchisor retains rights to (1) open anywhere, (2) license any brand anywhere, (3) use any distribution channel, (4) acquire competitors, (5) be acquired by competitors. Extremely broad reservation of rights.
ROFR within 1-mile radius if franchisee not in default. Exceptions for development areas, special venues, retail outlets, simplified concepts. 30 days to exercise.
Franchisor judgment: may make decisions based on readily available information and judgment of best interests. No requirement for reasonableness except where specifically stated.
Royalty: 4% of Gross Sales, weekly ACH by Wednesday.
BPF: 1% of GS (may increase to 2%), weekly ACH.
Late payment: interest up to 18% per annum + $100 service fee per occurrence. ACH decline treated as NSF.
Lost revenue damages on termination: NPV of royalties+BPF for lesser of 5 years or remaining term. Based on prior 12-month GS or system average. Due within 15 days.
Must secure site within 180 days of FA or FA may be terminated. Must obtain franchisor written approval before signing lease.
Franchisor must approve lease terms. Lease addendum required. Franchisee not third-party beneficiary of lease negotiations.
Must use designated architect and kitchen designer. Obtain all permits, licenses, liquor license. Construct per approved plans.
Must open within earlier of 18 months after lease signing or 24 months after FA. Failure = termination + forfeiture of franchise fee.
4 Designated Managers must complete 6-week training. If they cannot complete to satisfaction, FA may be terminated. Grand opening trainer provided free for 1st/2nd restaurant.
Must comply with System Standards. Franchisor may change specifications without consent and with no upper limit.
Managing Owner or Designated Managers must supervise full-time. DMs may not engage in any other significant business activity.
Franchisee may terminate only if franchisor fails to materially comply and doesn't cure within 90 days (or 180 days if cure is impossible).

Franchisor may terminate for extensive list of curable (10-30 day cure) and non-curable defaults. Non-curable includes: abandonment 3+ days, felony, 3 failures in 12 months, failure to pay any third party including landlord.

Broadly defined transfer includes any sale, assignment, gift, mortgage, pledge, security interest, or loss of possession/control. Prior written approval required. Extensive conditions including sign current FA, pay transfer fee, sign general release.
ROFR: franchisor has 30 days to match any third-party offer on same terms.
During term: no involvement in Competitive Business (10%+ breakfast revenue). Includes ownership, employment, lending, consulting. Applies to franchisee, owners, and Managing Owner.
Post-term: 2 years, 5-mile radius of premises AND 5-mile radius of any other Restaurant in the system. Same broad Competitive Business definition.

All entity owners must sign personal guaranty. Joint and several liability for all FA obligations. Spouse must consent in writing (binding marital estate assets). Guaranty survives until all obligations paid/performed.
Mandatory arbitration within 50 miles of franchisor's principal place of business (Eugene, OR). Costs split. Punitive damages waived. Jury trial waived.
Oregon governing law.
Forum selection: federal or state court nearest to franchisor's principal place of business.

Must maintain: property (replacement cost + 1 year loss of income), crime ($100K), liquor liability ($1M), EPL ($1M), cyber ($250K), food borne illness ($1M), auto ($1M), reputation restoration, workers comp. May change at any time. 30-day notice of cancellation. Waiver of subrogation. Primary/non-contributory.
Franchisee indemnifies franchisor, affiliates, and all their personnel against any claims arising from restaurant operations or conduct under the agreements.

Franchisor may enter premises and assume operations for up to 90-day increments, renewable up to 1 year aggregate. If due to non-compliance: 4% of GS + costs. If due to abandonment: franchisor retains all revenue.
Franchisor purchase option: upon Termination Event, franchisor may purchase restaurant at fair market value. Not a mandatory buyback — entirely at franchisor's option.
Must apply for government grants, non-recourse loans, or bail-outs for which franchisee qualifies if necessary to comply with obligations. Unusual provision requiring pursuit of government stimulus.
Franchisor may set maximum or minimum prices. If same max and min, effectively mandates pricing. Advertising policy may prohibit advertising prices different from suggested retail price.
